title: medicula lupulina subsp. jalasii
intro: Medicula lupulina subsp. jalasii, commonly known as Jalas' medicula, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Medicula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting immune function. Medicinal preparations of Medicula lupulina subsp. jalasii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to address various health issues.
